steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: Edge DNS is the building directory in every lobby of Akamai's global network. Visitors get pointed to the right floor instantly — never the wrong one.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
Edge DNS is authoritative DNS hosted across Akamai's anycast network. Features:

- Anycast — every query hits the nearest PoP
- DNSSEC support
- 100% uptime SLA on Enterprise tier
- API + Terraform provider for record management
- Pairs with GTM for traffic steering

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: Combine with GTM (Global Traffic Management) for geo / latency / weighted steering. Use Linode DNS Manager for simpler workloads — it's free and bundled with every Linode account.
"Edge DNS = authoritative anycast DNS. Linode DNS Manager = free, simpler. GTM = the steering layer on top."

quiz.question: You need weighted DNS routing across two regions. What do you use?

options.explain: Round-robin doesn't do weights or health checks.

options.text: GTM (Global Traffic Management) on top of Edge DNS
options.explain: GTM adds health-checked, weighted, geo-aware steering on top of authoritative DNS.
NodeBalancers are L4/L7 LBs, not global DNS steering.

quiz.question: You're prototyping. Which DNS service should you reach for first?

options.explain: Bundled with every Linode account — perfect for dev and prototypes. Upgrade to Edge DNS when you need anycast / DNSSEC.
You can, but why? DNS Manager is free.
Works but defeats the point of staying within Akamai.
